text.title: MLP Pipeline Rules and Gotchas
text.summary: Human-in-the-loop checkpoints, sequencing rules, and failure modes for the five-mode MLP pipeline.

rule.statement: A product manager must review and select which WOW features to accept after mode:propose, before running mode:update.
rule.rationale: mode:update writes to spec files autonomously. If the agent misinterprets a gap or proposes an incorrect MLP feature, the spec is modified without a review step. Chaining propose to update without human approval produces unreviewed spec changes.

rule.statement: Ensure spec files are committed to version control before running mode:update.
rule.rationale: The agent modifies files in place. Without version control, changes are not reviewable as a diff and are effectively unauditable.

section.title: When to Use MVP Scope Analyzer First

rule.statement: For a net-new product with no existing MVP, run faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent first, then hand its output to faion-mlp-agent starting at mode:propose.
rule.rationale: There is no existing MVP to analyze on a new product, so modes analyze and find-gaps are irrelevant. The MVP scope analyzer provides the baseline that mode:propose needs.

antipattern.description: Running mode:plan without engineering review, then treating its output as a sprint backlog.
antipattern.reason: mode:plan produces an implementation sequence but does not validate technical feasibility. It does not account for team capacity, technical debt, or infrastructure constraints.

antipattern.description: Applying MLP dimensions as a rigid checklist to every product.
antipattern.reason: Not every dimension applies to every product. For some products, Speed is irrelevant; for others, Personality is counterproductive. Use dimensions as a structured brainstorm framework, not a mandatory checklist.

antipattern.description: Using faion-mvp-scope-analyzer-agent output without validating against current competitor research.
antipattern.reason: The agent's training data may be outdated. In fast-moving markets (AI tooling, fintech), competitor feature sets change in months.

text.reference: Sequential pipeline coordinator for all five modes
